name: connect
context: Open SSH sessions or execute remote commands, including bastions, batch mode, and connection reuse
info: This skill wraps the core `ssh` client workflow for the jobs agents perform most often: interactive login, one-shot remote commands, bastion traversal with `-J` or `ProxyJump`, explicit identity selection, and batch-safe execution that fails instead of hanging on prompts. It also covers reusable connections via multiplexing and TTY control for commands like `sudo`. Use it whenever an agent must reach a remote machine directly rather than through an API.
body:
# Connect with ssh

Use `ssh` when the job is execution: open a shell, inspect a host, run a one-shot command, or step through a bastion to reach a private machine. Command-line flags are enough for ad hoc access, but repeated workflows should move into [[ssh/config]].

## Basic Patterns

```bash
ssh ops@host.example.com
ssh ops@host.example.com 'uname -a'
ssh -tt ops@host.example.com 'sudo journalctl -u api -n 50'
```

- No remote command means an interactive shell.
- Adding a remote command makes `ssh` run it and exit.
- `-tt` forces a TTY, which is often necessary for `sudo` or remote full-screen tools.

## Batch-Safe Execution

```bash
ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new ops@host.example.com 'uptime'
```

`BatchMode yes` disables password prompts, so the command fails fast if the key or agent is wrong. This is the default posture for autonomous agents.

## Bastions and Connection Reuse

```bash
ssh -J bastion.example.com ops@db.internal.example.com 'ps aux | grep postgres'
ssh -O check prod-api
ssh -O exit prod-api
```

`-J` is the command-line shortcut for `ProxyJump`. When `ControlMaster` is enabled in [[ssh/config]], `ssh -O` lets you inspect or stop the shared master connection.

sections.title: Interactive and one-shot sessions
sections.body: Use `ssh user@host` for an interactive shell and `ssh user@host command` for one-shot execution. `-t` or `-tt` forces TTY allocation; `-T` disables it. Explicit `-i keyfile`, `-p port`, and `-F config` are useful when you need to override defaults from [[ssh/config]].

sections.title: Non-interactive automation
sections.body: `BatchMode yes` prevents password and passphrase prompts. Combine it with a valid key from [[ssh/keys]] or [[ssh/agent]] and a scoped host policy such as `StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new` or `ask`. This keeps automation deterministic and makes failures visible to the caller instead of stalling.

sections.title: Bastions, ProxyJump, and reuse
sections.body: `-J jump-host` or `ProxyJump` routes a connection through one or more bastions. Multiplexing with `ControlMaster`, `ControlPath`, and `ControlPersist` allows later sessions to reuse one authenticated connection. Inspect that state with `ssh -O check host` and stop it with `ssh -O exit host`. See [[ssh/config]] for persistent setup.

scripts.name: Run SSH command
scripts.description: Open an interactive SSH session or execute a one-shot command on a remote host

SSH_KEY_PATH.description: Private key path passed to ssh -i

SSH_COMMAND.description: Remote command to execute; omit for an interactive shell

SSH_PROXY_JUMP.description: Jump host or chain passed to ssh -J

SSH_CONFIG_FILE.description: Alternate ssh config file passed with -F

SSH_BATCH_MODE.description: Set to true to pass BatchMode=yes

SSH_STRICT_HOST_KEY_CHECKING.description: Host-key policy: yes, ask, accept-new, no, or off

SSH_TTY.description: Set to true to force TTY allocation with -tt

SSH_DISABLE_TTY.description: Set to true to disable TTY allocation with -T

SSH_EXTRA_ARGS.description: Extra ssh arguments, one per line

command.description: Remote command to execute; omit for interactive mode

identityFile.description: Path to the private key file

proxyJump.description: Jump host or comma-separated jump chain

mode.description: Whether a remote command was provided

depends.reason: Public-key auth is the normal automation path for SSH
Bastions, aliases, and multiplexing are commonly defined in config
